title: algorithmacy-lab: exact IIT-4.0 Φ applied to organizational coordination, with an AI-assisted research protocol
description:
An open computational lab applying exact IIT-4.0 Φ, computed with PyPhi, to organizational coordination theory, with an AI-assisted research protocol that takes a question to a finished quantitative paper.

The lab (org_frontier) classifies a coordination form as dyadic when its cause-effect structure factors across the worker–system–counterpart partition (demanding literacy) and triadic when it stays irreducible (demanding algorithmacy), and runs a 134-experiment hypothesis-testing program over the construct — including a worked question taken end to end through the automated pipeline to a full paper. A six-stage protocol (review, deep research, hypotheses fixed before computing, methods, run against the exact-Φ instrument, paper) is documented and runnable.

The foundations arc validates the instrument first: on systems small enough that exact Φ is computable, it tests what tracks integrated information — empirical proxies and theoretical candidate measures against exact Φ, scalar Φ versus the full cause-effect structure, a learned surrogate and its size-extrapolation, causal emergence versus Φ, and the data-style ΦID estimation gap.

Results are in-silico; a validation gap separates them from claims about real organizations. See the repository README, org_frontier/, and foundations/SYNTHESIS.md.
